agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Smart Route Optimization is a logistics feature that automates driver route assignment at shift start and dynamically recalculates routes in response to real-time events. It spans multiple bounded contexts: route planning, fleet eligibility, delivery lifecycle, and customer communication. The goal is to maximize delivery throughput per driver shift while keeping customers informed and respecting physical/regulatory constraints on vehicle-delivery matching.","bounded_context":"This feature touches four bounded contexts: (1) Route Planning — owns route calculation, optimization strategy, recalculation triggers, and stop-time estimates; (2) Fleet Management — owns driver profiles, vehicle classes, licensing constraints, and shift lifecycle; (3) Delivery Management — owns delivery lifecycle (pending, in-transit, failed, completed), zone assignment, and delivery attributes (size, weight, class requirement); (4) Customer Notification — owns notification triggers, delivery proximity windows, and customer communication preferences. Each context should own its own data and communicate via domain events.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Optimized Route","definition":"An ordered sequence of delivery stops assigned to a driver for a given shift, calculated to minimize total travel time or distance (optimization goal unspecified — see escalation)."},{"term":"Zone","definition":"A geographic area used to partition pending deliveries and assign them to drivers whose shift is associated with that zone."},{"term":"Route Recalculation","definition":"The act of recomputing the optimized route for an active driver shift in response to a defined trigger event (new delivery, failed delivery, or manual deviation)."},{"term":"Manual Deviation","definition":"When a driver's actual path or stop sequence diverges from the system-suggested optimized route. The precise threshold or definition of deviation is unspecified."},{"term":"Next 3 Stops Window","definition":"A proximity notification trigger: when a delivery is within the next 3 planned stops on the driver's active route, the customer receives a notification."},{"term":"Licensed Vehicle Class","definition":"A classification of vehicle type (e.g., motorcycle, van, truck, freight) that determines which categories of deliveries a driver is eligible to carry. Drivers hold licenses for specific classes."},{"term":"Delivery Stop Time","definition":"The estimated duration a driver spends at each delivery stop, defaulting to 4 minutes but overridable by the operations team at the zone level."},{"term":"Pending Delivery","definition":"A delivery that has been created and is awaiting assignment to a driver route within its zone."},{"term":"Failed Delivery","definition":"A delivery attempt that was unsuccessful, triggering route recalculation to remove it from the active sequence and potentially reschedule."},{"term":"Shift","definition":"A bounded work period for a driver, during which they are assigned a zone and an optimized route of deliveries."},{"term":"Operations Team","definition":"Internal stakeholders with authority to override system defaults such as stop-time estimates on a per-zone basis."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"When a driver starts their shift, the system must automatically generate an optimized route from all pending deliveries in the driver's assigned zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational workflow trigger — the optimization algorithm and assignment strategy are policy decisions that could change (e.g., batch assignment vs. real-time).","testable_assertion":"GIVEN pending deliveries exist in zone X, WHEN a driver assigned to zone X starts their shift, THEN the system produces an ordered route containing only pending deliveries from zone X."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A driver's route must be recalculated when a new delivery is added to their active zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Recalculation policy could change — e.g., batch recalculations every N minutes instead of immediate. This is an operational policy.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver has an active route in zone X, WHEN a new pending delivery is created in zone X, THEN the driver's route is recalculated to include the new delivery."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A driver's route must be recalculated when a delivery on their route is marked as failed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Recalculation on failure is policy — could be deferred or handled differently. Not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver has an active route containing delivery D, WHEN delivery D is marked as failed, THEN the route is recalculated excluding delivery D."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A driver's route must be recalculated when the driver manually deviates from the suggested route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Deviation response is policy. The system could alternatively flag but not recalculate, or offer the driver a choice.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver has an active route, WHEN the system detects manual deviation from that route, THEN the route is recalculated from the driver's current position."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Customers must be notified when their delivery enters the 'next 3 stops' window on the driver's active route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The notification window size (3) is a business policy that could be adjusted. The notification itself is a preference, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer has a pending delivery, WHEN that delivery becomes one of the next 3 stops on the assigned driver's route, THEN the customer receives a notification."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A driver must never be assigned a delivery that exceeds their licensed vehicle class capacity or type.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a physical and potentially regulatory constraint. A motorcycle cannot physically carry freight. Violating this makes the delivery unexecutable and may violate transportation regulations. The system is in a corrupt state if this assignment exists.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver is licensed for vehicle class M, WHEN the route optimizer considers delivery D requiring vehicle class F where F is not compatible with M, THEN delivery D must be excluded from that driver's route."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The default estimated time per delivery stop is 4 minutes, overridable by the operations team per zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a configurable operational parameter — a policy decision that the operations team can change at will.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN zone X has no override configured, WHEN route time is estimated, THEN each stop uses 4 minutes. GIVEN zone X has an override of 6 minutes, WHEN route time is estimated, THEN each stop in zone X uses 6 minutes."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"A route must only contain deliveries from the driver's assigned zone.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Zone isolation is a structural integrity constraint. Cross-zone assignments would violate the partitioning model and create routing impossibilities, double-assignments, and operational chaos.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver is assigned to zone X, THEN every delivery on their route must belong to zone X and no delivery from zone Y may appear on the route."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A driver assigned to zone X starts their shift and there are 10 pending deliveries in zone X","when":"The shift-start event is processed","then":"The system generates an optimized route containing exactly the eligible pending deliveries from zone X, ordered by the optimization algorithm, and assigns it to the driver"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A driver has an active route in zone X with 5 remaining stops","when":"A new delivery is created in zone X","then":"The driver's route is recalculated to include the new delivery in the optimal position, and any customers newly entering the next-3-stops window are notified"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A driver has an active route with delivery D at stop 4 of 8","when":"Delivery D is marked as failed","then":"The route is recalculated excluding delivery D, remaining deliveries are re-optimized, and customer notification windows are re-evaluated"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A driver has an active optimized route","when":"The system detects the driver has deviated from the suggested route","then":"The route is recalculated from the driver's current position using remaining undelivered stops"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A customer's delivery is currently at position 5 on a driver's route","when":"A route recalculation moves that delivery to position 3 (within the next-3-stops window)","then":"The customer receives a notification that their delivery is approaching"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A motorcycle-class driver starts their shift in a zone with both parcel deliveries and freight shipments","when":"The route is generated","then":"Only parcel deliveries compatible with motorcycle class are included; freight shipments are excluded from this driver's route"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"The operations team has set a 6-minute stop time override for zone X","when":"A route is calculated for a driver in zone X","then":"The ETA calculations use 6 minutes per stop instead of the default 4 minutes"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A customer was previously notified (delivery was in next-3-stops window)","when":"A route recalculation moves their delivery outside the next-3-stops window","then":"The system handles the notification state appropriately (behavior unspecified — see escalation)"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A driver starts their shift in a zone with zero pending deliveries","when":"The shift-start event is processed","then":"The driver is assigned an empty route and the system begins monitoring for new deliveries in the zone to trigger recalculation"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"Multiple drivers are active in the same zone","when":"A new delivery arrives in the zone","then":"The system determines which driver should receive the delivery and recalculates only the affected driver's route"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"No pending deliveries at shift start","expected_behavior":"Driver receives an empty route. System monitors zone for new deliveries and triggers route creation when one arrives."},{"scenario":"All deliveries in a zone require a vehicle class no active driver is licensed for","expected_behavior":"Deliveries remain in pending state unassigned. System should alert operations team that deliveries are unserviceable with current fleet."},{"scenario":"Route recalculation triggered simultaneously by multiple events (new delivery + failed delivery)","expected_behavior":"Events should be processed to produce a single coherent recalculation, not two sequential recalculations that could race."},{"scenario":"Customer notified as next-3-stops but route recalculation pushes them back beyond 3","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — spec does not define whether a retraction or update notification is sent. Escalated."},{"scenario":"Driver deviates, route recalculates, driver deviates again immediately","expected_behavior":"System must handle rapid successive recalculations without thrashing. A debounce or cooldown mechanism may be needed."},{"scenario":"A delivery is added to a zone where multiple drivers are active","expected_behavior":"Spec does not define how to choose which driver receives the new delivery. Needs assignment strategy (nearest, least loaded, etc.)."},{"scenario":"Driver's shift ends mid-route with deliveries remaining","expected_behavior":"Unspecified. Remaining deliveries must return to pending pool for reassignment or be handed off."},{"scenario":"Operations team changes zone stop-time override while routes are active","expected_behavior":"Unclear whether active routes recalculate ETAs immediately or only on next recalculation trigger."},{"scenario":"A delivery is reassigned from a failed attempt — does it re-enter the pending pool?","expected_behavior":"Spec says route recalculates excluding failed delivery but does not define the failed delivery's next lifecycle state."},{"scenario":"Zone boundary: delivery is geographically on the border of two zones","expected_behavior":"Spec does not define how zone membership is determined or how boundary cases are resolved."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Route optimization directly impacts delivery throughput per driver shift — even a 10% improvement in route efficiency at scale translates to significant fuel savings, more deliveries per shift, and reduced overtime costs. Incorrect vehicle-class matching could result in failed deliveries and wasted driver trips.","operational":"Real-time recalculation on every zone event (new delivery, failure, deviation) creates significant computational load. The operations team's ability to override stop times per zone provides necessary local tuning. However, the recalculation frequency policy needs careful design to avoid system thrashing.","user":"Customer notification at the next-3-stops window significantly improves delivery experience by providing timely, actionable updates. However, if route recalculations frequently shift delivery positions, customers may receive confusing or contradictory notifications.","risk":"The vehicle-class constraint (BR-6) is safety-critical — a motorcycle assigned a heavy freight shipment is physically dangerous and potentially a regulatory violation. The deviation detection mechanism has privacy implications (continuous GPS tracking). Notification reliability affects customer trust.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Drivers","impact":"Receive automated routes at shift start, must follow suggested paths or trigger recalculations. Reduced decision-making burden but less autonomy. Vehicle class constraints protect them from unsafe assignments."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Receive proximity notifications improving delivery predictability. Risk of notification confusion if routes recalculate frequently."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Gain per-zone stop-time override capability. Need visibility into route optimization performance and unassignable deliveries."},{"group":"Dispatchers","impact":"Role potentially reduced or changed — automated route assignment replaces manual dispatch decisions. Need to understand how multi-driver zone assignment works."},{"group":"Fleet Management","impact":"Vehicle class data must be accurate and current. Licensing data becomes a critical system dependency."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The spec describes a coherent feature but leaves several operationally critical details ambiguous: what constitutes deviation, how multi-driver zones are handled, what optimization objective is used, and how notification state is managed across recalculations. These are not contradictions — the spec is simply vague on implementation-critical points. The core rules (vehicle class constraint, recalculation triggers, notification window) are clear.","high_confidence_areas":["Vehicle class constraint is a hard invariant — physically and regulatorily non-negotiable","Three explicit recalculation triggers are well-defined: new delivery, failed delivery, manual deviation","Customer notification at next-3-stops window is a clear business rule","Default 4-minute stop time with per-zone operations override is clear","Zone isolation for route assignment is clear"],"low_confidence_areas":["Optimization objective (time vs. distance vs. priority) not stated","What constitutes 'manual deviation' from suggested route — GPS distance threshold? Skipping a stop? Taking a different road?","How shift-end with remaining deliveries is handled","Whether notification retraction occurs when recalculation pushes a delivery outside the 3-stop window","How real-time stop-time override changes affect already-active routes"],"assumptions":["Each driver is assigned to exactly one zone per shift","Zone assignment is predetermined before shift start (not dynamically reassigned)","Vehicle class compatibility is a binary match, not a graduated scale","The 'next 3 stops' window is evaluated after every route change, not on a polling interval","Failed deliveries are removed from the route but their downstream lifecycle is handled by a separate bounded context"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec says routes are 'optimized' but does not define the optimization objective. Minimizing total distance, minimizing total time, prioritizing time-sensitive deliveries, and balancing driver workload are all valid interpretations that produce materially different routes.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Customers","Operations Team"],"options":["Optimize for minimum total travel time","Optimize for minimum total distance","Optimize for delivery priority/SLA compliance","Multi-objective optimization with configurable weights"],"recommendation":"Define a primary optimization objective (likely minimum travel time) with secondary constraints (e.g., SLA deadlines). Allow the operations team to configure weights per zone."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Manual deviation from the suggested route' is not defined. It could mean: (a) driver takes a different road to the same next stop, (b) driver skips a stop and goes to a later one, (c) driver goes to a location not on the route. Each interpretation implies a different detection mechanism and recalculation response.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team"],"options":["Define deviation as visiting stops out of order","Define deviation as GPS path divergence beyond a threshold distance","Define deviation as any stop-skip or off-route location visit","Combine: skip detection + GPS geofence departure"],"recommendation":"Define deviation as the driver completing a stop out of the suggested sequence OR departing the geofenced corridor to the next stop by more than a configurable threshold. Road-level rerouting (e.g., avoiding traffic) should NOT trigger recalculation."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"When multiple drivers are active in the same zone and a new delivery arrives, the spec does not define which driver receives the delivery. This is a critical assignment strategy question.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Customers","Dispatchers"],"options":["Assign to nearest driver by current position","Assign to driver with fewest remaining stops (load balancing)","Assign to driver whose route insertion causes least total time increase","Let dispatchers manually assign"],"recommendation":"Use least-total-time-increase as primary heuristic — this preserves overall route optimality. Fall back to load balancing as a tiebreaker."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Customer notification lifecycle across recalculations is undefined. If a customer was notified (delivery in next-3-stops) but recalculation pushes them back to stop 5, should the system send an updated notification, retract the original, or stay silent?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers"],"options":["Send an update notification explaining the delay","Stay silent to avoid notification spam","Send retraction only if delivery moves more than N positions back"],"recommendation":"Send an updated notification if the delivery moves outside the window after being notified. Customer trust requires honest communication even when the news is negative. Include a new ETA in the update."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Driver shift-end behavior with remaining deliveries is unspecified. Remaining deliveries need a defined handoff process — return to pending pool, transfer to another active driver, or hold for next shift.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Customers","Operations Team","Dispatchers"],"options":["Return undelivered items to pending pool for reassignment","Auto-transfer to another active driver in same zone","Hold for driver's next shift","Alert operations for manual decision"],"recommendation":"Return undelivered items to the pending pool and trigger recalculation for any remaining active drivers in the zone. Notify affected customers of the delay."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Smart Route Optimization is a logistics feature that automates driver route assignment at shift start and dynamically recalculates routes in response to real-time events. It spans multiple bounded contexts: route planning, fleet eligibility, delivery lifecycle, and customer communication. The goal is to maximize delivery throughput per driver shift while keeping customers informed and respecting physical/regulatory constraints on vehicle-delivery matching.

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this logistics domain scenario carefully.

This is a "Smart Route Optimization" feature. Let me break down the bounded contexts, business rules, and produce a full domain analysis.

Key bounded contexts I see:
1. Route Optimization - route calculation, recalculation triggers
2. Fleet/Driver Management - vehicle class licensing, shift management
3. Delivery Management - delivery lifecycle, zones
4. Customer Notification - the "next 3 stops" window

Let me identify business rules, invariants, edge cases, and any ambiguities.


I'm noticing several key ambiguities that need clarification: what exactly counts as deviating from a suggested route, how zones are defined and whether they're static or dynamic, who manages the vehicle class licensing mappings, whether the "next 3 stops" notification is a fixed number or configurable, and what optimization criteria are being prioritized. There's also the question of how to handle mid-shift route recalculations when customers have already been notified, and how the 4-minute per-stop override interacts with zone-level timing rules.

The main blocker here is that multiple interpretations are possible for nearly every requirement, so I need to flag these as ambiguities and produce a full analysis of the spec.

Skipping a stop? Taking a different road?","How shift-end with remaining deliveries is handled","Whether notification retraction occurs when recalculation pushes a delivery outside the 3-stop window","How real-time stop-time override changes affect already-active routes"],"assumptions":["Each driver is assigned to exactly one zone per shift","Zone assignment is predetermined before shift start (not dynamically reassigned)","Vehicle class compatibility is a binary match, not a graduated scale","The 'next 3 stops' window is evaluated after every route change, not on a polling interval","Failed deliveries are removed from the route but their downstream lifecycle is handled by a separate bounded context"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec says routes are 'optimized' but does not define the optimization objective. Minimizing total distance, minimizing total time, prioritizing time-sensitive deliveries, and balancing driver workload are all valid interpretations that produce materially different routes.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Customers","Operations Team"],"options":["Optimize for minimum total travel time","Optimize for minimum total distance","Optimize for delivery priority/SLA compliance","Multi-objective optimization with configurable weights"],"recommendation":"Define a primary optimization objective (likely minimum travel time) with secondary constraints (e.g., SLA deadlines). Allow the operations team to configure weights per zone."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Manual deviation from the suggested route' is not defined. It could mean: (a) driver takes a different road to the same next stop, (b) driver skips a stop and goes to a later one, (c) driver goes to a location not on the route. Each interpretation implies a different detection mechanism and recalculation response.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team"],"options":["Define deviation as visiting stops out of order","Define deviation as GPS path divergence beyond a threshold distance","Define deviation as any stop-skip or off-route location visit","Combine: skip detection + GPS geofence departure"],"recommendation":"Define deviation as the driver completing a stop out of the suggested sequence OR departing the geofenced corridor to the next stop by more than a configurable threshold. Road-level rerouting (e.g., avoiding traffic) should NOT trigger recalculation."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"When multiple drivers are active in the same zone and a new delivery arrives, the spec does not define which driver receives the delivery. This is a critical assignment strategy question.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Customers","Dispatchers"],"options":["Assign to nearest driver by current position","Assign to driver with fewest remaining stops (load balancing)","Assign to driver whose route insertion causes least total time increase","Let dispatchers manually assign"],"recommendation":"Use least-total-time-increase as primary heuristic — this preserves overall route optimality. Fall back to load balancing as a tiebreaker."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Customer notification lifecycle across recalculations is undefined. If a customer was notified (delivery in next-3-stops) but recalculation pushes them back to stop 5, should the system send an updated notification, retract the original, or stay silent?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers"],"options":["Send an update notification explaining the delay","Stay silent to avoid notification spam","Send retraction only if delivery moves more than N positions back"],"recommendation":"Send an updated notification if the delivery moves outside the window after being notified. Customer trust requires honest communication even when the news is negative. Include a new ETA in the update."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Driver shift-end behavior with remaining deliveries is unspecified. Remaining deliveries need a defined handoff process — return to pending pool, transfer to another active driver, or hold for next shift.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Customers","Operations Team","Dispatchers"],"options":["Return undelivered items to pending pool for reassignment","Auto-transfer to another active driver in same zone","Hold for driver's next shift","Alert operations for manual decision"],"recommendation":"Return undelivered items to the pending pool and trigger recalculation for any remaining active drivers in the zone. Notify affected customers of the delay."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

trace.result: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Smart Route Optimization is a logistics feature that automates driver route assignment at shift start and dynamically recalculates routes in response to real-time events. It spans multiple bounded contexts: route planning, fleet eligibility, delivery lifecycle, and customer communication. The goal is to maximize delivery throughput per driver shift while keeping customers informed and respecting physical/regulatory constraints on vehicle-delivery matching.","bounded_context":"This feature touches four bounded contexts: (1) Route Planning — owns route calculation, optimization strategy, recalculation triggers, and stop-time estimates; (2) Fleet Management — owns driver profiles, vehicle classes, licensing constraints, and shift lifecycle; (3) Delivery Management — owns delivery lifecycle (pending, in-transit, failed, completed), zone assignment, and delivery attributes (size, weight, class requirement); (4) Customer Notification — owns notification triggers, delivery proximity windows, and customer communication preferences. Each context should own its own data and communicate via domain events.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Optimized Route","definition":"An ordered sequence of delivery stops assigned to a driver for a given shift, calculated to minimize total travel time or distance (optimization goal unspecified — see escalation)."},{"term":"Zone","definition":"A geographic area used to partition pending deliveries and assign them to drivers whose shift is associated with that zone."},{"term":"Route Recalculation","definition":"The act of recomputing the optimized route for an active driver shift in response to a defined trigger event (new delivery, failed delivery, or manual deviation)."},{"term":"Manual Deviation","definition":"When a driver's actual path or stop sequence diverges from the system-suggested optimized route. The precise threshold or definition of deviation is unspecified."},{"term":"Next 3 Stops Window","definition":"A proximity notification trigger: when a delivery is within the next 3 planned stops on the driver's active route, the customer receives a notification."},{"term":"Licensed Vehicle Class","definition":"A classification of vehicle type (e.g., motorcycle, van, truck, freight) that determines which categories of deliveries a driver is eligible to carry. Drivers hold licenses for specific classes."},{"term":"Delivery Stop Time","definition":"The estimated duration a driver spends at each delivery stop, defaulting to 4 minutes but overridable by the operations team at the zone level."},{"term":"Pending Delivery","definition":"A delivery that has been created and is awaiting assignment to a driver route within its zone."},{"term":"Failed Delivery","definition":"A delivery attempt that was unsuccessful, triggering route recalculation to remove it from the active sequence and potentially reschedule."},{"term":"Shift","definition":"A bounded work period for a driver, during which they are assigned a zone and an optimized route of deliveries."},{"term":"Operations Team","definition":"Internal stakeholders with authority to override system defaults such as stop-time estimates on a per-zone basis."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"When a driver starts their shift, the system must automatically generate an optimized route from all pending deliveries in the driver's assigned zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational workflow trigger — the optimization algorithm and assignment strategy are policy decisions that could change (e.g., batch assignment vs. real-time).","testable_assertion":"GIVEN pending deliveries exist in zone X, WHEN a driver assigned to zone X starts their shift, THEN the system produces an ordered route containing only pending deliveries from zone X."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A driver's route must be recalculated when a new delivery is added to their active zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Recalculation policy could change — e.g., batch recalculations every N minutes instead of immediate. This is an operational policy.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver has an active route in zone X, WHEN a new pending delivery is created in zone X, THEN the driver's route is recalculated to include the new delivery."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A driver's route must be recalculated when a delivery on their route is marked as failed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Recalculation on failure is policy — could be deferred or handled differently. Not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver has an active route containing delivery D, WHEN delivery D is marked as failed, THEN the route is recalculated excluding delivery D."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A driver's route must be recalculated when the driver manually deviates from the suggested route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Deviation response is policy. The system could alternatively flag but not recalculate, or offer the driver a choice.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver has an active route, WHEN the system detects manual deviation from that route, THEN the route is recalculated from the driver's current position."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Customers must be notified when their delivery enters the 'next 3 stops' window on the driver's active route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The notification window size (3) is a business policy that could be adjusted. The notification itself is a preference, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer has a pending delivery, WHEN that delivery becomes one of the next 3 stops on the assigned driver's route, THEN the customer receives a notification."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A driver must never be assigned a delivery that exceeds their licensed vehicle class capacity or type.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a physical and potentially regulatory constraint. A motorcycle cannot physically carry freight. Violating this makes the delivery unexecutable and may violate transportation regulations. The system is in a corrupt state if this assignment exists.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver is licensed for vehicle class M, WHEN the route optimizer considers delivery D requiring vehicle class F where F is not compatible with M, THEN delivery D must be excluded from that driver's route."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The default estimated time per delivery stop is 4 minutes, overridable by the operations team per zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a configurable operational parameter — a policy decision that the operations team can change at will.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN zone X has no override configured, WHEN route time is estimated, THEN each stop uses 4 minutes. GIVEN zone X has an override of 6 minutes, WHEN route time is estimated, THEN each stop in zone X uses 6 minutes."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"A route must only contain deliveries from the driver's assigned zone.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Zone isolation is a structural integrity constraint. Cross-zone assignments would violate the partitioning model and create routing impossibilities, double-assignments, and operational chaos.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver is assigned to zone X, THEN every delivery on their route must belong to zone X and no delivery from zone Y may appear on the route."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A driver assigned to zone X starts their shift and there are 10 pending deliveries in zone X","when":"The shift-start event is processed","then":"The system generates an optimized route containing exactly the eligible pending deliveries from zone X, ordered by the optimization algorithm, and assigns it to the driver"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A driver has an active route in zone X with 5 remaining stops","when":"A new delivery is created in zone X","then":"The driver's route is recalculated to include the new delivery in the optimal position, and any customers newly entering the next-3-stops window are notified"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A driver has an active route with delivery D at stop 4 of 8","when":"Delivery D is marked as failed","then":"The route is recalculated excluding delivery D, remaining deliveries are re-optimized, and customer notification windows are re-evaluated"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A driver has an active optimized route","when":"The system detects the driver has deviated from the suggested route","then":"The route is recalculated from the driver's current position using remaining undelivered stops"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A customer's delivery is currently at position 5 on a driver's route","when":"A route recalculation moves that delivery to position 3 (within the next-3-stops window)","then":"The customer receives a notification that their delivery is approaching"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A motorcycle-class driver starts their shift in a zone with both parcel deliveries and freight shipments","when":"The route is generated","then":"Only parcel deliveries compatible with motorcycle class are included; freight shipments are excluded from this driver's route"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"The operations team has set a 6-minute stop time override for zone X","when":"A route is calculated for a driver in zone X","then":"The ETA calculations use 6 minutes per stop instead of the default 4 minutes"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A customer was previously notified (delivery was in next-3-stops window)","when":"A route recalculation moves their delivery outside the next-3-stops window","then":"The system handles the notification state appropriately (behavior unspecified — see escalation)"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A driver starts their shift in a zone with zero pending deliveries","when":"The shift-start event is processed","then":"The driver is assigned an empty route and the system begins monitoring for new deliveries in the zone to trigger recalculation"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"Multiple drivers are active in the same zone","when":"A new delivery arrives in the zone","then":"The system determines which driver should receive the delivery and recalculates only the affected driver's route"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"No pending deliveries at shift start","expected_behavior":"Driver receives an empty route. System monitors zone for new deliveries and triggers route creation when one arrives."},{"scenario":"All deliveries in a zone require a vehicle class no active driver is licensed for","expected_behavior":"Deliveries remain in pending state unassigned. System should alert operations team that deliveries are unserviceable with current fleet."},{"scenario":"Route recalculation triggered simultaneously by multiple events (new delivery + failed delivery)","expected_behavior":"Events should be processed to produce a single coherent recalculation, not two sequential recalculations that could race."},{"scenario":"Customer notified as next-3-stops but route recalculation pushes them back beyond 3","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — spec does not define whether a retraction or update notification is sent. Escalated."},{"scenario":"Driver deviates, route recalculates, driver deviates again immediately","expected_behavior":"System must handle rapid successive recalculations without thrashing. A debounce or cooldown mechanism may be needed."},{"scenario":"A delivery is added to a zone where multiple drivers are active","expected_behavior":"Spec does not define how to choose which driver receives the new delivery. Needs assignment strategy (nearest, least loaded, etc.)."},{"scenario":"Driver's shift ends mid-route with deliveries remaining","expected_behavior":"Unspecified. Remaining deliveries must return to pending pool for reassignment or be handed off."},{"scenario":"Operations team changes zone stop-time override while routes are active","expected_behavior":"Unclear whether active routes recalculate ETAs immediately or only on next recalculation trigger."},{"scenario":"A delivery is reassigned from a failed attempt — does it re-enter the pending pool?","expected_behavior":"Spec says route recalculates excluding failed delivery but does not define the failed delivery's next lifecycle state."},{"scenario":"Zone boundary: delivery is geographically on the border of two zones","expected_behavior":"Spec does not define how zone membership is determined or how boundary cases are resolved."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Route optimization directly impacts delivery throughput per driver shift — even a 10% improvement in route efficiency at scale translates to significant fuel savings, more deliveries per shift, and reduced overtime costs. Incorrect vehicle-class matching could result in failed deliveries and wasted driver trips.","operational":"Real-time recalculation on every zone event (new delivery, failure, deviation) creates significant computational load. The operations team's ability to override stop times per zone provides necessary local tuning. However, the recalculation frequency policy needs careful design to avoid system thrashing.","user":"Customer notification at the next-3-stops window significantly improves delivery experience by providing timely, actionable updates. However, if route recalculations frequently shift delivery positions, customers may receive confusing or contradictory notifications.","risk":"The vehicle-class constraint (BR-6) is safety-critical — a motorcycle assigned a heavy freight shipment is physically dangerous and potentially a regulatory violation. The deviation detection mechanism has privacy implications (continuous GPS tracking). Notification reliability affects customer trust.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Drivers","impact":"Receive automated routes at shift start, must follow suggested paths or trigger recalculations. Reduced decision-making burden but less autonomy. Vehicle class constraints protect them from unsafe assignments."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Receive proximity notifications improving delivery predictability. Risk of notification confusion if routes recalculate frequently."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Gain per-zone stop-time override capability. Need visibility into route optimization performance and unassignable deliveries."},{"group":"Dispatchers","impact":"Role potentially reduced or changed — automated route assignment replaces manual dispatch decisions. Need to understand how multi-driver zone assignment works."},{"group":"Fleet Management","impact":"Vehicle class data must be accurate and current. Licensing data becomes a critical system dependency."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The spec describes a coherent feature but leaves several operationally critical details ambiguous: what constitutes deviation, how multi-driver zones are handled, what optimization objective is used, and how notification state is managed across recalculations. These are not contradictions — the spec is simply vague on implementation-critical points. The core rules (vehicle class constraint, recalculation triggers, notification window) are clear.","high_confidence_areas":["Vehicle class constraint is a hard invariant — physically and regulatorily non-negotiable","Three explicit recalculation triggers are well-defined: new delivery, failed delivery, manual deviation","Customer notification at next-3-stops window is a clear business rule","Default 4-minute stop time with per-zone operations override is clear","Zone isolation for route assignment is clear"],"low_confidence_areas":["Optimization objective (time vs. distance vs. priority) not stated","What constitutes 'manual deviation' from suggested route — GPS distance threshold? Skipping a stop? Taking a different road?","How shift-end with remaining deliveries is handled","Whether notification retraction occurs when recalculation pushes a delivery outside the 3-stop window","How real-time stop-time override changes affect already-active routes"],"assumptions":["Each driver is assigned to exactly one zone per shift","Zone assignment is predetermined before shift start (not dynamically reassigned)","Vehicle class compatibility is a binary match, not a graduated scale","The 'next 3 stops' window is evaluated after every route change, not on a polling interval","Failed deliveries are removed from the route but their downstream lifecycle is handled by a separate bounded context"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec says routes are 'optimized' but does not define the optimization objective. Minimizing total distance, minimizing total time, prioritizing time-sensitive deliveries, and balancing driver workload are all valid interpretations that produce materially different routes.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Customers","Operations Team"],"options":["Optimize for minimum total travel time","Optimize for minimum total distance","Optimize for delivery priority/SLA compliance","Multi-objective optimization with configurable weights"],"recommendation":"Define a primary optimization objective (likely minimum travel time) with secondary constraints (e.g., SLA deadlines). Allow the operations team to configure weights per zone."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Manual deviation from the suggested route' is not defined. It could mean: (a) driver takes a different road to the same next stop, (b) driver skips a stop and goes to a later one, (c) driver goes to a location not on the route. Each interpretation implies a different detection mechanism and recalculation response.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team"],"options":["Define deviation as visiting stops out of order","Define deviation as GPS path divergence beyond a threshold distance","Define deviation as any stop-skip or off-route location visit","Combine: skip detection + GPS geofence departure"],"recommendation":"Define deviation as the driver completing a stop out of the suggested sequence OR departing the geofenced corridor to the next stop by more than a configurable threshold. Road-level rerouting (e.g., avoiding traffic) should NOT trigger recalculation."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"When multiple drivers are active in the same zone and a new delivery arrives, the spec does not define which driver receives the delivery. This is a critical assignment strategy question.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Customers","Dispatchers"],"options":["Assign to nearest driver by current position","Assign to driver with fewest remaining stops (load balancing)","Assign to driver whose route insertion causes least total time increase","Let dispatchers manually assign"],"recommendation":"Use least-total-time-increase as primary heuristic — this preserves overall route optimality. Fall back to load balancing as a tiebreaker."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Customer notification lifecycle across recalculations is undefined. If a customer was notified (delivery in next-3-stops) but recalculation pushes them back to stop 5, should the system send an updated notification, retract the original, or stay silent?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers"],"options":["Send an update notification explaining the delay","Stay silent to avoid notification spam","Send retraction only if delivery moves more than N positions back"],"recommendation":"Send an updated notification if the delivery moves outside the window after being notified. Customer trust requires honest communication even when the news is negative. Include a new ETA in the update."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Driver shift-end behavior with remaining deliveries is unspecified. Remaining deliveries need a defined handoff process — return to pending pool, transfer to another active driver, or hold for next shift.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Customers","Operations Team","Dispatchers"],"options":["Return undelivered items to pending pool for reassignment","Auto-transfer to another active driver in same zone","Hold for driver's next shift","Alert operations for manual decision"],"recommendation":"Return undelivered items to the pending pool and trigger recalculation for any remaining active drivers in the zone. Notify affected customers of the delay."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
